steps.message:
# Task: Generate Developer Onboarding Guide for Istio Control Plane

**Repository:** istio/istio
**Output:** Write your guide to `/workspace/documentation.md`

## Objective

Produce a developer onboarding guide for Istio's control plane. This guide should help a new contributor understand how to build Istio, navigate the codebase, and make their first contribution.

## Scope

Your guide must cover all of the following:

### 1. Build Prerequisites
- Required Go version, tools, and environment variables
- How to clone the repository with all submodules
- How to build the core control plane binaries (`pilot-discovery`, `istiod`)
- How to run the test suite

### 2. Architecture Overview
- Istio's control plane components and their responsibilities (Pilot, Citadel, Galley — now merged into istiod)
- The xDS protocol and how istiod pushes configuration to Envoy proxies
- Key packages: `pilot/pkg/`, `security/pkg/`, `galley/pkg/`
- How the service registry integrates with Kubernetes

### 3. First Contribution Workflow
- How to find good first issues
- How to run linters and pre-commit checks
- How to write and run unit tests for a change
- How to submit a PR (required reviewers, CI gates)

## Quality Bar

- Reference specific Makefile targets, Go packages, or scripts
- Architecture section must explain at least one data flow end-to-end (e.g., service discovery to xDS push)
- Do not fabricate commands — verify against actual Makefile and scripts in the repo

## Anti-Requirements

- Do not simply reproduce the README
- Do not include Kubernetes operator/installation instructions (focus on developer workflow only)

I'll help you create a comprehensive developer onboarding guide for Istio's control plane. Let me start by exploring the repository structure to understand the build system, architecture, and actual commands.

# WARNING: DO NOT EDIT, THIS FILE IS PROBABLY A COPY
#
# The original version of this file is located in the https://github.com/istio/common-files repo.
# If you're looking at this file in a different repo and want to make a change, please go to the
# common-files repo, make the change there and check it in. Then come back to this repo and run
# "make update-common".

# Copyright Istio Authors
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

SHELL := /usr/bin/env bash

# allow optional per-repo overrides
-include Makefile.overrides.mk

# Set the environment variable BUILD_WITH_CONTAINER to use a container
# to build the repo. The only dependencies in this mode are to have make and
# docker. If you'd rather build with a local tool chain instead, you'll need to
# figure out all the tools you need in your environment to make that work.
export BUILD_WITH_CONTAINER ?= 0

ifeq ($(BUILD_WITH_CONTAINER),1)

# An export free of arguments in a Makefile places all variables in the Makefile into the
# environment. This is needed to allow overrides from Makefile.overrides.mk.
export

RUN = ./common/scripts/run.sh

MAKE_DOCKER = $(RUN) make --no-print-directory -e -f Makefile.core.mk

%:
	@$(MAKE_DOCKER) $@

default:
	@$(MAKE_DOCKER)

shell:
	@$(RUN) /bin/bash

.PHONY: default shell

else

# If we are not in build container, we need a workaround to get environment properly set
# Write to file, then include
$(shell mkdir -p out)
$(shell $(shell pwd)/common/scripts/setup_env.sh envfile > out/.env)
include out/.env
# An export free of arguments in a Makefile places all variables in the Makefile into the
# environment. This behavior may be surprising to many that use shell often, which simply
# displays the existing environment
export

export GOBIN ?= $(GOPATH)/bin
include Makefile.core.mk

endif

1→## Copyright Istio Authors
     2→##
     3→## Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     4→## you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     5→## You may obtain a copy of the License at
     6→##
     7→##     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     8→##
     9→## Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    10→## distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    11→## WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    12→## See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    13→## limitations under the License.
    14→
    15→# Our build tools, post jammy, breaks old versions of docker.
    16→# These old versions are surprisingly common, so build in a check here
    17→define warning
    18→Docker version is too old, please upgrade to a newer version.
    19→endef
    20→ifneq ($(findstring google,$(HOSTNAME)),)
    21→warning+=Googlers: go/installdocker\#the-version-of-docker-thats-installed-is-old-eg-1126
    22→endif
    23→# The old docker issue manifests as not being able to run *any* binary. So we can test
    24→# by trying to run a trivial program and ensuring it actually ran. If not, emit our warning.
    25→# Note: we cannot do anything like $(shell docker version) to check, since that would also fail.
    26→CAN_RUN := $(shell echo "can I run echo")
    27→ifeq ($(CAN_RUN),)
    28→$(error $(warning))
    29→endif
    30→
    31→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→# Global Variables
    33→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→ISTIO_GO := $(shell dirname $(realpath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
    35→export ISTIO_GO
    36→SHELL := /bin/bash -o pipefail
    37→
    38→# Version can be defined:
    39→# (1) in a $VERSION shell variable, which takes precedence; or
    40→# (2) in the VERSION file, in which we will append "-dev" to it
    41→ifeq ($(VERSION),)
    42→VERSION_FROM_FILE := $(shell cat VERSION)
    43→ifeq ($(VERSION_FROM_FILE),)
    44→$(error VERSION not detected. Make sure it's stored in the VERSION file or defined in VERSION variable)
    45→endif
    46→VERSION := $(VERSION_FROM_FILE)-dev
    47→endif
    48→
    49→export VERSION
    50→
    51→# Base version of Istio image to use
    52→BASE_VERSION ?= master-2026-02-04T19-02-20
    53→ISTIO_BASE_REGISTRY ?= gcr.io/istio-release
    54→
    55→export GO111MODULE ?= on
    56→export GOPROXY ?= https://proxy.golang.org
    57→export GOSUMDB ?= sum.golang.org
    58→
    59→# If GOPATH is not set by the env, set it to a sane value
    60→GOPATH ?= $(shell cd ${ISTIO_GO}/../../..; pwd)
    61→export GOPATH
    62→
    63→# If GOPATH is made up of several paths, use the first one for our targets in this Makefile
    64→GO_TOP := $(shell echo ${GOPATH} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    65→export GO_TOP
    66→
    67→GO ?= go
    68→
    69→GOARCH_LOCAL := $(TARGET_ARCH)
    70→GOOS_LOCAL := $(TARGET_OS)
    71→
    72→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→# Output control
    74→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→# Invoke make VERBOSE=1 to enable echoing of the command being executed
    76→export VERBOSE ?= 0
    77→# Place the variable Q in front of a command to control echoing of the command being executed.
    78→Q = $(if $(filter 1,$VERBOSE),,@)
    79→# Use the variable H to add a header (equivalent to =>) to informational output
    80→H = $(shell printf "\033[34;1m=>\033[0m")
    81→
    82→ifeq ($(origin DEBUG), undefined)
    83→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
    84→else ifeq ($(DEBUG),0)
    85→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
    86→else
    87→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=debug
    88→  export GCFLAGS:=all=-N -l
    89→  $(info $(H) Build with debugger information)
    90→endif
    91→
    92→# Optional file including user-specific settings (HUB, TAG, etc)
    93→-include .istiorc.mk
    94→
    95→# Environment for tests, the directory containing istio and deps binaries.
    96→# Typically same as GOPATH/bin, so tests work seamlessly with IDEs.
    97→
    98→export ISTIO_BIN=$(GOBIN)
    99→
   100→# If we are running in the Linux build container on non Linux hosts, we add the
   101→# linux binaries to the build dependencies, BUILD_DEPS, which can be added to other targets
   102→# that would need the Linux binaries (ex. tests).
   103→BUILD_DEPS:=
   104→ifeq ($(IN_BUILD_CONTAINER),1)
   105→  ifneq ($(GOOS_LOCAL),"linux")
   106→    BUILD_DEPS += build-linux
   107→  endif
   108→endif
   109→
   110→export ARTIFACTS ?= $(TARGET_OUT)
   111→export JUNIT_OUT ?= $(ARTIFACTS)/junit.xml
   112→export REPO_ROOT := $(shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
   113→
   114→# Make directories needed by the build system
   115→$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX))
   116→$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/logs)
   117→$(shell mkdir -p $(dir $(JUNIT_OUT)))
   118→
   119→# Need separate target for init:
   120→$(TARGET_OUT):
   121→	@mkdir -p $@
   122→
   123→# If the hub is not explicitly set, use default to istio.
   124→HUB ?=istio
   125→ifeq ($(HUB),)
   126→  $(error "HUB cannot be empty")
   127→endif
   128→
   129→# For dockerx builds, allow HUBS which is a space separated list of hubs. Default to HUB.
   130→HUBS ?= $(HUB)
   131→
   132→# If tag not explicitly set in users' .istiorc.mk or command line, default to the git sha.
   133→TAG ?= $(shell git rev-parse --verify HEAD)
   134→ifeq ($(TAG),)
   135→  $(error "TAG cannot be empty")
   136→endif
   137→
   138→PULL_POLICY ?= IfNotPresent
   139→ifeq ($(TAG),latest)
   140→  PULL_POLICY = Always
   141→endif
   142→ifeq ($(PULL_POLICY),)
   143→  $(error "PULL_POLICY cannot be empty")
   144→endif
   145→
   146→PROW_ARTIFACTS_BASE ?= https://gcsweb.istio.io/gcs/istio-prow
   147→
   148→include tools/proto/proto.mk
   149→
   150→.PHONY: default
   151→default: init build test
   152→
   153→.PHONY: init
   154→# Downloads envoy, based on the SHA defined in the base pilot Dockerfile
   155→init: $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init init-ztunnel-rs
   156→	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/logs
   157→	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/release
   158→
   159→# I tried to make this dependent on what I thought was the appropriate
   160→# lock file, but it caused the rule for that file to get run (which
   161→# seems to be about obtaining a new version of the 3rd party libraries).
   162→$(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init: bin/init.sh istio.deps | $(TARGET_OUT)
   163→	@# Add a retry, as occasionally we see transient connection failures to GCS
   164→	@# Like `curl: (56) OpenSSL SSL_read: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL, errno 104`
   165→	TARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) ISTIO_BIN=$(ISTIO_BIN) GOOS_LOCAL=$(GOOS_LOCAL) bin/retry.sh SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL bin/init.sh
   166→	touch $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init

   189→.PHONY: precommit format lint

   191→# Target run by the pre-commit script, to automate formatting and lint

   199→ifeq ($(DEBUG),1)
   200→# gobuild script uses custom linker flag to set the variables.
   201→RELEASE_LDFLAGS=''
   202→else
   203→RELEASE_LDFLAGS='-extldflags -static -s -w'
   204→endif
   205→
   206→# List of all binaries to build
   207→# We split the binaries into "agent" binaries and standard ones. This corresponds to build "agent".
   208→# This allows conditional compilation to avoid pulling in costly dependencies to the agent, such as XDS and k8s.
   209→AGENT_BINARIES:=./pilot/cmd/pilot-agent
   210→STANDARD_BINARIES:=./istioctl/cmd/istioctl \
   211→  ./pilot/cmd/pilot-discovery \
   212→  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/client \
   213→  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/server \
   214→  ./samples/extauthz/cmd/extauthz
   215→
   216→CNI_BINARIES:=./cni/cmd/istio-cni \
   217→	./cni/cmd/install-cni
   218→# These are binaries that require Linux to build, and should
   219→# be skipped on other platforms. Notably this includes the current Linux-only Istio CNI plugin
   220→LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES:=$(CNI_BINARIES) \
   221→  $(AGENT_BINARIES)
   222→
   223→BINARIES:=$(STANDARD_BINARIES) $(AGENT_BINARIES) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)
   224→
   225→# List of binaries that have their size tested
   226→RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES:=pilot-discovery pilot-agent istioctl envoy ztunnel client server
   227→
   228→# agent: enables agent-specific files. Usually this is used to trim dependencies where they would be hard to trim through standard refactoring
   229→# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
   230→# not set vtprotobuf: this adds some performance improvement, but at a binary cost increase that is not worth it for the agent
   231→# notrace: helps with https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/56636 and reduces binary size
   232→AGENT_TAGS=agent,disable_pgv,grpcnotrace,retrynotrace
   233→# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
   234→# vtprotobuf: enables optimized protobuf marshalling.
   235→STANDARD_TAGS=vtprotobuf,disable_pgv

   237→.PHONY: build
   238→build: depend ## Builds all go binaries.
   239→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
   240→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(AGENT_BINARIES)
   241→
   242→# The build-linux target is responsible for building binaries used within containers.
   243→# This target should be expanded upon as we add more Linux architectures: i.e. build-arm64.
   244→# Then a new build target can be created such as build-container-bin that builds these
   245→# various platform images.
   246→.PHONY: build-linux
   247→build-linux: depend
   248→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
   249→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)
   250→
   251→.PHONY: build-cni
   252→build-cni: depend
   253→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(CNI_BINARIES)
   254→
   255→# Create targets for TARGET_OUT_LINUX/binary
   256→# There are two use cases here:
   257→# * Building all docker images (generally in CI). In this case we want to build everything at once, so they share work
   258→# * Building a single docker image (generally during dev). In this case we just want to build the single binary alone
   259→BUILD_ALL ?= true
   260→define build-linux
   261→.PHONY: $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1))
   262→ifeq ($(BUILD_ALL),true)
   263→$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): build-linux
   264→	@:
   265→else
   266→$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)
   267→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(2) $(1)
   268→endif
   269→endef
   270→
   271→$(foreach bin,$(STANDARD_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(STANDARD_TAGS))))
   272→$(foreach bin,$(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(AGENT_TAGS))))

   274→# Create helper targets for each binary, like "pilot-discovery"
   275→# As an optimization, these still build everything
   276→$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),$(shell basename $(bin))): build

   278→# if we are on linux already, then this rule is handled by build-linux above, which handles BUILD_ALL variable
   279→$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),${LOCAL_OUT}/$(shell basename $(bin))): build

   327→CHARTS = gateway default ztunnel base "gateways/istio-ingress" "gateways/istio-egress" "istio-control/istio-discovery" istio-cni
   328→copy-templates:

   330→
   331→	# gateway charts

   333→	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/ingress/egress/g' {} \;
   334→	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/Ingress/Egress/g' {} \;
   335→	if [ -f ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml ]; then \

   337→		sed -i -e 's/istio-ingress/istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \

   339→		sed -i -e 's/istio: ingressgateway/istio: egressgateway/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
   340→	fi
   341→
   342→	# copy istio-discovery values, but apply some local customizations
   343→	warning=$$(cat manifests/helm-profiles/warning-edit.txt | sed ':a;N;$$!ba;s/\n/\\n/g') ; \

So we can test\n# by trying to run a trivial program and ensuring it actually ran. If not, emit our warning.\n# Note: we cannot do anything like $(shell docker version) to check, since that would also fail.\nCAN_RUN := $(shell echo \"can I run echo\")\nifeq ($(CAN_RUN),)\n$(error $(warning))\nendif\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Global Variables\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\nISTIO_GO := $(shell dirname $(realpath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))\nexport ISTIO_GO\nSHELL := /bin/bash -o pipefail\n\n# Version can be defined:\n# (1) in a $VERSION shell variable, which takes precedence; or\n# (2) in the VERSION file, in which we will append \"-dev\" to it\nifeq ($(VERSION),)\nVERSION_FROM_FILE := $(shell cat VERSION)\nifeq ($(VERSION_FROM_FILE),)\n$(error VERSION not detected. Make sure it's stored in the VERSION file or defined in VERSION variable)\nendif\nVERSION := $(VERSION_FROM_FILE)-dev\nendif\n\nexport VERSION\n\n# Base version of Istio image to use\nBASE_VERSION ?= master-2026-02-04T19-02-20\nISTIO_BASE_REGISTRY ?= gcr.io/istio-release\n\nexport GO111MODULE ?= on\nexport GOPROXY ?= https://proxy.golang.org\nexport GOSUMDB ?= sum.golang.org\n\n# If GOPATH is not set by the env, set it to a sane value\nGOPATH ?= $(shell cd ${ISTIO_GO}/../../..; pwd)\nexport GOPATH\n\n# If GOPATH is made up of several paths, use the first one for our targets in this Makefile\nGO_TOP := $(shell echo ${GOPATH} | cut -d ':' -f1)\nexport GO_TOP\n\nGO ?= go\n\nGOARCH_LOCAL := $(TARGET_ARCH)\nGOOS_LOCAL := $(TARGET_OS)\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Output control\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Invoke make VERBOSE=1 to enable echoing of the command being executed\nexport VERBOSE ?= 0\n# Place the variable Q in front of a command to control echoing of the command being executed.\nQ = $(if $(filter 1,$VERBOSE),,@)\n# Use the variable H to add a header (equivalent to =>) to informational output\nH = $(shell printf \"\\033[34;1m=>\\033[0m\")\n\nifeq ($(origin DEBUG), undefined)\n  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release\nelse ifeq ($(DEBUG),0)\n  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release\nelse\n  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=debug\n  export GCFLAGS:=all=-N -l\n  $(info $(H) Build with debugger information)\nendif\n\n# Optional file including user-specific settings (HUB, TAG, etc)\n-include .istiorc.mk\n\n# Environment for tests, the directory containing istio and deps binaries.\n# Typically same as GOPATH/bin, so tests work seamlessly with IDEs.\n\nexport ISTIO_BIN=$(GOBIN)\n\n# If we are running in the Linux build container on non Linux hosts, we add the\n# linux binaries to the build dependencies, BUILD_DEPS, which can be added to other targets\n# that would need the Linux binaries (ex. tests).\nBUILD_DEPS:=\nifeq ($(IN_BUILD_CONTAINER),1)\n  ifneq ($(GOOS_LOCAL),\"linux\")\n    BUILD_DEPS += build-linux\n  endif\nendif\n\nexport ARTIFACTS ?= $(TARGET_OUT)\nexport JUNIT_OUT ?= $(ARTIFACTS)/junit.xml\nexport REPO_ROOT := $(shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel)\n\n# Make directories needed by the build system\n$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX))\n$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/logs)\n$(shell mkdir -p $(dir $(JUNIT_OUT)))\n\n# Need separate target for init:\n$(TARGET_OUT):\n\t@mkdir -p $@\n\n# If the hub is not explicitly set, use default to istio.\nHUB ?=istio\nifeq ($(HUB),)\n  $(error \"HUB cannot be empty\")\nendif\n\n# For dockerx builds, allow HUBS which is a space separated list of hubs. Default to HUB.\nHUBS ?= $(HUB)\n\n# If tag not explicitly set in users' .istiorc.mk or command line, default to the git sha.\nTAG ?= $(shell git rev-parse --verify HEAD)\nifeq ($(TAG),)\n  $(error \"TAG cannot be empty\")\nendif\n\nPULL_POLICY ?= IfNotPresent\nifeq ($(TAG),latest)\n  PULL_POLICY = Always\nendif\nifeq ($(PULL_POLICY),)\n  $(error \"PULL_POLICY cannot be empty\")\nendif\n\nPROW_ARTIFACTS_BASE ?= https://gcsweb.istio.io/gcs/istio-prow\n\ninclude tools/proto/proto.mk\n\n.PHONY: default\ndefault: init build test\n\n.PHONY: init\n# Downloads envoy, based on the SHA defined in the base pilot Dockerfile\ninit: $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init init-ztunnel-rs\n\t@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/logs\n\t@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/release\n\n# I tried to make this dependent on what I thought was the appropriate\n# lock file, but it caused the rule for that file to get run (which\n# seems to be about obtaining a new version of the 3rd party libraries).\n$(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init: bin/init.sh istio.deps | $(TARGET_OUT)\n\t@# Add a retry, as occasionally we see transient connection failures to GCS\n\t@# Like `curl: (56) OpenSSL SSL_read: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL, errno 104`\n\tTARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) ISTIO_BIN=$(ISTIO_BIN) GOOS_LOCAL=$(GOOS_LOCAL) bin/retry.sh SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL bin/init.sh\n\ttouch $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init\n\n.PHONY: init-ztunnel-rs\ninit-ztunnel-rs:\n\tTARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) bin/build_ztunnel.sh\n\n# Pull dependencies such as envoy\ndepend: init | $(TARGET_OUT)\n\nDIRS_TO_CLEAN := $(TARGET_OUT)\nDIRS_TO_CLEAN += $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)\n\n$(OUTPUT_DIRS):\n\t@mkdir -p $@\n\n.PHONY: ${GEN_CERT}\nGEN_CERT := ${ISTIO_BIN}/generate_cert\n${GEN_CERT}:\n\tGOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) && GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) && common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./security/tools/generate_cert\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: precommit\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n.PHONY: precommit format lint\n\n# Target run by the pre-commit script, to automate formatting and lint\n# If pre-commit script is not used, please run this manually.\nprecommit: format lint\n\nformat: fmt ## Auto formats all code. This should be run before sending a PR.\n\nfmt: format-go format-python tidy-go\n\nifeq ($(DEBUG),1)\n# gobuild script uses custom linker flag to set the variables.\nRELEASE_LDFLAGS=''\nelse\nRELEASE_LDFLAGS='-extldflags -static -s -w'\nendif\n\n# List of all binaries to build\n# We split the binaries into \"agent\" binaries and standard ones. This corresponds to build \"agent\".\n# This allows conditional compilation to avoid pulling in costly dependencies to the agent, such as XDS and k8s.\nAGENT_BINARIES:=./pilot/cmd/pilot-agent\nSTANDARD_BINARIES:=./istioctl/cmd/istioctl \\\n  ./pilot/cmd/pilot-discovery \\\n  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/client \\\n  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/server \\\n  ./samples/extauthz/cmd/extauthz\n\nCNI_BINARIES:=./cni/cmd/istio-cni \\\n\t./cni/cmd/install-cni\n# These are binaries that require Linux to build, and should\n# be skipped on other platforms. Notably this includes the current Linux-only Istio CNI plugin\nLINUX_AGENT_BINARIES:=$(CNI_BINARIES) \\\n  $(AGENT_BINARIES)\n\nBINARIES:=$(STANDARD_BINARIES) $(AGENT_BINARIES) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)\n\n# List of binaries that have their size tested\nRELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES:=pilot-discovery pilot-agent istioctl envoy ztunnel client server\n\n# agent: enables agent-specific files. Usually this is used to trim dependencies where they would be hard to trim through standard refactoring\n# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos\n# not set vtprotobuf: this adds some performance improvement, but at a binary cost increase that is not worth it for the agent\n# notrace: helps with https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/56636 and reduces binary size\nAGENT_TAGS=agent,disable_pgv,grpcnotrace,retrynotrace\n# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos\n# vtprotobuf: enables optimized protobuf marshalling.\nSTANDARD_TAGS=vtprotobuf,disable_pgv\n\n.PHONY: build\nbuild: depend ## Builds all go binaries.\n\tGOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)\n\tGOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(AGENT_BINARIES)\n\n# The build-linux target is responsible for building binaries used within containers.\n# This target should be expanded upon as we add more Linux architectures: i.e. build-arm64.\n# Then a new build target can be created such as build-container-bin that builds these\n# various platform images.\n.PHONY: build-linux\nbuild-linux: depend\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)\n\n.PHONY: build-cni\nbuild-cni: depend\n\tGOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(CNI_BINARIES)\n\n# Create targets for TARGET_OUT_LINUX/binary\n# There are two use cases here:\n# * Building all docker images (generally in CI). In this case we want to build everything at once, so they share work\n# * Building a single docker image (generally during dev). In this case we just want to build the single binary alone\nBUILD_ALL ?= true\ndefine build-linux\n.PHONY: $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1))\nifeq ($(BUILD_ALL),true)\n$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): build-linux\n\t@:\nelse\n$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(2) $(1)\nendif\nendef\n\n$(foreach bin,$(STANDARD_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(STANDARD_TAGS))))\n$(foreach bin,$(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(AGENT_TAGS))))\n\n# Create helper targets for each binary, like \"pilot-discovery\"\n# As an optimization, these still build everything\n$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),$(shell basename $(bin))): build\nifneq ($(TARGET_OUT_LINUX),$(LOCAL_OUT))\n# if we are on linux already, then this rule is handled by build-linux above, which handles BUILD_ALL variable\n$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),${LOCAL_OUT}/$(shell basename $(bin))): build\nendif\n\nMARKDOWN_LINT_ALLOWLIST=localhost:8080,storage.googleapis.com/istio-artifacts/pilot/,http://ratings.default.svc.cluster.local:9080/ratings\n\nlint-helm-global:\n\tfind manifests -name 'Chart.yaml' -print0 | ${XARGS} -L 1 dirname | xargs -r helm lint\n\nlint: lint-python lint-copyright-banner lint-scripts lint-go lint-dockerfiles lint-markdown lint-yaml lint-licenses lint-helm-global ## Runs all linters.\n\t@bin/check_samples.sh\n\t@testlinter\n\t@envvarlinter istioctl pilot security\n\ngo-gen:\n\t@mkdir -p /tmp/bin\n\t@PATH=\"${PATH}\":/tmp/bin go generate ./...\n\nrefresh-goldens:\n\tREFRESH_GOLDEN=true go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./operator/... \\\n\t\t./pkg/bootstrap/... \\\n\t\t./pkg/kube/inject/... \\\n\t\t./pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/... \\\n\t\t./pilot/pkg/security/authz/builder/... \\\n\t\t./pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller/ambient/... \\\n\t\t./cni/pkg/iptables/... \\\n\t\t./cni/pkg/plugin/... \\\n\t\t./istioctl/pkg/workload/... \\\n\t\t./istioctl/pkg/writer/envoy/configdump/... \\\n\t\t./istioctl/pkg/writer/ztunnel/configdump/...\n\nupdate-golden: refresh-goldens\n\ngen-addons:\n\tmanifests/addons/gen.sh\n\ngen: \\\n\tmod-download-go \\\n\tgo-gen \\\n\tmirror-licenses \\\n\tformat \\\n\tupdate-crds \\\n\tproto \\\n\tcopy-templates \\\n\tgen-addons \\\n\tupdate-golden ## Update all generated code.\n\ngen-check: gen check-clean-repo\n\nCHARTS = gateway default ztunnel base \"gateways/istio-ingress\" \"gateways/istio-egress\" \"istio-control/istio-discovery\" istio-cni\ncopy-templates:\n\trm manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/*\n\n\t# gateway charts\n\tcp -r manifests/charts/gateways/istio-ingress/templates/* manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates\n\tfind ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name \"*.yaml\" ! -name \"networkpolicy.yaml\" -exec sed -i -e 's/ingress/egress/g' {} \\;\n\tfind ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name \"*.yaml\" ! -name \"networkpolicy.yaml\" -exec sed -i -e 's/Ingress/Egress/g' {} \\;\n\tif [ -f ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml ]; then \\\n\t\tsed -i -e 's/\"IngressGateways\"/\"EgressGateways\"/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \\\n\t\tsed -i -e 's/istio-ingress/istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \\\n\t\tsed -i -e 's/app: istio-ingress/app: istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \\\n\t\tsed -i -e 's/istio: ingressgateway/istio: egressgateway/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \\\n\tfi\n\n\t# copy istio-discovery values, but apply some local customizations\n\twarning=$$(cat manifests/helm-profiles/warning-edit.txt | sed ':a;N;$$!ba;s/\\n/\\\\n/g') ; \\\n\tfor chart in $(CHARTS) ; do \\\n\t    rm -rf manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-*.yaml ; \\\n\t\tfor profile in manifests/helm-profiles/*.yaml ; do \\\n\t\t\tsed \"1s|^|$${warning}\\n\\n|\" $$profile > manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-$$(basename $$profile) ; \\\n\t\tdone; \\\n\t\t[[ \"$$chart\" == \"ztunnel\" ]] || [[ \"$$chart\" == \"gateway\" ]] && flatten=\"true\" || flatten=\"false\" ; \\\n\t\tcat manifests/zzz_profile.yaml | \\\n\t\t  sed \"s/FLATTEN_GLOBALS_REPLACEMENT/$${flatten}/g\" \\\n\t\t  > manifests/charts/$$chart/templates/zzz_profile.yaml ; \\\n\tdone\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: go build\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Non-static istioctl targets. These are typically a build artifact.\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64:\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7:\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=arm GOARM=7 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64:\n\tGOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx:\n\tGOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64:\n\tGOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe:\n\tGOOS=windows LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl\n\n# generate the istioctl completion files\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl\n\t${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion bash > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash\n\n${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl\n\t${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion zsh > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl\n\n.PHONY: binaries-test\nbinaries-test:\n\tgo test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./tests/binary/... -v --base-dir ${TARGET_OUT} --binaries=\"$(RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES)\"\n\n# istioctl-all makes all of the non-static istioctl executables for each supported OS\n.PHONY: istioctl-all\nistioctl-all: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64 \\\n\t${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx \\\n\t${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64 \\\n\t${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe\n\n.PHONY: istioctl.completion\nistioctl.completion: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl\n\n# istioctl-install builds then installs istioctl into $GOPATH/BIN\n# Used for debugging istioctl during dev work\n.PHONY: istioctl-install-container\nistioctl-install-container: istioctl\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: test\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n.PHONY: test\n\n# This target sets JUNIT_REPORT to the location of the  go-junit-report binary.\n# This binary is provided in the build container. If it is not found, the build\n# container is not being used, so ask the user to install go-junit-report.\nJUNIT_REPORT := $(shell which go-junit-report 2> /dev/null || echo \"${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report\")\n\n${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report:\n\t@echo \"go-junit-report was not found in the build environment.\"\n\t@echo \"Please install go-junit-report (ex. go install github.com/jstemmer/go-junit-report@latest)\"\n\t@exit 1\n\n# This is just an alias for racetest now\ntest: racetest ## Runs all unit tests\n\n# For now, keep a minimal subset. This can be expanded in the future.\nBENCH_TARGETS ?= ./pilot/...\n\nPKG ?= ./...\n.PHONY: racetest\nracetest: $(JUNIT_REPORT)\n\tgo test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ${T} -tags=assert -race $(PKG) 2>&1 | tee >($(JUNIT_REPORT) > $(JUNIT_OUT))\n\n.PHONY: benchtest\nbenchtest: $(JUNIT_REPORT) ## Runs all benchmarks\n\tprow/benchtest.sh run $(BENCH_TARGETS)\n\tprow/benchtest.sh compare\n\nreport-benchtest:\n\tprow/benchtest.sh report\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: clean\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n.PHONY: clean\n\nclean: ## Cleans all the intermediate files and folders previously generated.\n\trm -rf $(DIRS_TO_CLEAN)\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: docker\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n.PHONY: push\n\n# for now docker is limited to Linux compiles - why ?\ninclude tools/istio-docker.mk\n\npush: docker.push ## Build and push docker images to registry defined by $HUB and $TAG\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: environment and tools\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n.PHONY: show.env show.goenv\n\nshow.env: ; $(info $(H) environment variables...)\n\t$(Q) printenv\n\nshow.goenv: ; $(info $(H) go environment...)\n\t$(Q) $(GO) version\n\t$(Q) $(GO) env\n\n# show makefile variables. Usage: make show.<variable-name>\nshow.%: ; $(info $* $(H) $($*))\n\t$(Q) true\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: custom resource definitions\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nupdate-crds:\n\tbin/update_crds.sh\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: artifacts and distribution\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# deb, rpm, etc packages\ninclude tools/packaging/packaging.mk\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: integration tests\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\ninclude tests/integration/tests.mk\n\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Target: bookinfo sample\n#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport BOOKINFO_VERSION ?= 1.18.0\n\n.PHONY: bookinfo.build bookinfo.push\n\nbookinfo.build:\n\t@prow/buildx-create\n\t@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh\n\nbookinfo.push: MULTI_ARCH=true\nbookinfo.push:\n\t@prow/buildx-create\n\t@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh --push\n\ninclude common/Makefile.common.mk\n", "numLines": 504, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 504}}

## Copyright Istio Authors
##
## Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
## you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
## You may obtain a copy of the License at
##
##     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
##
## Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
## distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
## WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
## See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
## limitations under the License.

# Our build tools, post jammy, breaks old versions of docker.
# These old versions are surprisingly common, so build in a check here
define warning
Docker version is too old, please upgrade to a newer version.
endef
ifneq ($(findstring google,$(HOSTNAME)),)
warning+=Googlers: go/installdocker\#the-version-of-docker-thats-installed-is-old-eg-1126
endif
# The old docker issue manifests as not being able to run *any* binary. So we can test
# by trying to run a trivial program and ensuring it actually ran. If not, emit our warning.
# Note: we cannot do anything like $(shell docker version) to check, since that would also fail.
CAN_RUN := $(shell echo "can I run echo")
ifeq ($(CAN_RUN),)
$(error $(warning))
endif

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Global Variables
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ISTIO_GO := $(shell dirname $(realpath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
export ISTIO_GO
SHELL := /bin/bash -o pipefail

# Version can be defined:
# (1) in a $VERSION shell variable, which takes precedence; or
# (2) in the VERSION file, in which we will append "-dev" to it
ifeq ($(VERSION),)
VERSION_FROM_FILE := $(shell cat VERSION)
ifeq ($(VERSION_FROM_FILE),)
$(error VERSION not detected. Make sure it's stored in the VERSION file or defined in VERSION variable)
endif
VERSION := $(VERSION_FROM_FILE)-dev
endif

export VERSION

# Base version of Istio image to use
BASE_VERSION ?= master-2026-02-04T19-02-20
ISTIO_BASE_REGISTRY ?= gcr.io/istio-release

export GO111MODULE ?= on
export GOPROXY ?= https://proxy.golang.org
export GOSUMDB ?= sum.golang.org

# If GOPATH is not set by the env, set it to a sane value
GOPATH ?= $(shell cd ${ISTIO_GO}/../../..; pwd)
export GOPATH

# If GOPATH is made up of several paths, use the first one for our targets in this Makefile
GO_TOP := $(shell echo ${GOPATH} | cut -d ':' -f1)
export GO_TOP

GO ?= go

GOARCH_LOCAL := $(TARGET_ARCH)
GOOS_LOCAL := $(TARGET_OS)

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Output control
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Invoke make VERBOSE=1 to enable echoing of the command being executed
export VERBOSE ?= 0
# Place the variable Q in front of a command to control echoing of the command being executed.
Q = $(if $(filter 1,$VERBOSE),,@)
# Use the variable H to add a header (equivalent to =>) to informational output
H = $(shell printf "\033[34;1m=>\033[0m")

ifeq ($(origin DEBUG), undefined)
  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
else ifeq ($(DEBUG),0)
  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
else
  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=debug
  export GCFLAGS:=all=-N -l
  $(info $(H) Build with debugger information)
endif

# Optional file including user-specific settings (HUB, TAG, etc)
-include .istiorc.mk

# Environment for tests, the directory containing istio and deps binaries.
# Typically same as GOPATH/bin, so tests work seamlessly with IDEs.

export ISTIO_BIN=$(GOBIN)

# If we are running in the Linux build container on non Linux hosts, we add the
# linux binaries to the build dependencies, BUILD_DEPS, which can be added to other targets
# that would need the Linux binaries (ex. tests).
BUILD_DEPS:=
ifeq ($(IN_BUILD_CONTAINER),1)
  ifneq ($(GOOS_LOCAL),"linux")
    BUILD_DEPS += build-linux
  endif
endif

export ARTIFACTS ?= $(TARGET_OUT)
export JUNIT_OUT ?= $(ARTIFACTS)/junit.xml
export REPO_ROOT := $(shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel)

# Make directories needed by the build system
$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX))
$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/logs)
$(shell mkdir -p $(dir $(JUNIT_OUT)))

# Need separate target for init:
$(TARGET_OUT):
	@mkdir -p $@

# If the hub is not explicitly set, use default to istio.
HUB ?=istio
ifeq ($(HUB),)
  $(error "HUB cannot be empty")
endif

# For dockerx builds, allow HUBS which is a space separated list of hubs. Default to HUB.
HUBS ?= $(HUB)

# If tag not explicitly set in users' .istiorc.mk or command line, default to the git sha.
TAG ?= $(shell git rev-parse --verify HEAD)
ifeq ($(TAG),)
  $(error "TAG cannot be empty")
endif

PULL_POLICY ?= IfNotPresent
ifeq ($(TAG),latest)
  PULL_POLICY = Always
endif
ifeq ($(PULL_POLICY),)
  $(error "PULL_POLICY cannot be empty")
endif

PROW_ARTIFACTS_BASE ?= https://gcsweb.istio.io/gcs/istio-prow

include tools/proto/proto.mk

.PHONY: default
default: init build test

.PHONY: init
# Downloads envoy, based on the SHA defined in the base pilot Dockerfile
init: $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init init-ztunnel-rs
	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/logs
	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/release

# I tried to make this dependent on what I thought was the appropriate
# lock file, but it caused the rule for that file to get run (which
# seems to be about obtaining a new version of the 3rd party libraries).
$(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init: bin/init.sh istio.deps | $(TARGET_OUT)
	@# Add a retry, as occasionally we see transient connection failures to GCS
	@# Like `curl: (56) OpenSSL SSL_read: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL, errno 104`
	TARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) ISTIO_BIN=$(ISTIO_BIN) GOOS_LOCAL=$(GOOS_LOCAL) bin/retry.sh SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL bin/init.sh
	touch $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init

.PHONY: init-ztunnel-rs
init-ztunnel-rs:
	TARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) bin/build_ztunnel.sh

# Pull dependencies such as envoy
depend: init | $(TARGET_OUT)

DIRS_TO_CLEAN := $(TARGET_OUT)
DIRS_TO_CLEAN += $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)

$(OUTPUT_DIRS):
	@mkdir -p $@

.PHONY: ${GEN_CERT}
GEN_CERT := ${ISTIO_BIN}/generate_cert
${GEN_CERT}:
	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) && GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) && common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./security/tools/generate_cert

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: precommit
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: precommit format lint

# Target run by the pre-commit script, to automate formatting and lint
# If pre-commit script is not used, please run this manually.
precommit: format lint

format: fmt ## Auto formats all code. This should be run before sending a PR.

fmt: format-go format-python tidy-go

ifeq ($(DEBUG),1)
# gobuild script uses custom linker flag to set the variables.
RELEASE_LDFLAGS=''
else
RELEASE_LDFLAGS='-extldflags -static -s -w'
endif

# List of all binaries to build
# We split the binaries into "agent" binaries and standard ones. This corresponds to build "agent".
# This allows conditional compilation to avoid pulling in costly dependencies to the agent, such as XDS and k8s.
AGENT_BINARIES:=./pilot/cmd/pilot-agent
STANDARD_BINARIES:=./istioctl/cmd/istioctl \
  ./pilot/cmd/pilot-discovery \
  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/client \
  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/server \
  ./samples/extauthz/cmd/extauthz

CNI_BINARIES:=./cni/cmd/istio-cni \
	./cni/cmd/install-cni
# These are binaries that require Linux to build, and should
# be skipped on other platforms. Notably this includes the current Linux-only Istio CNI plugin
LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES:=$(CNI_BINARIES) \
  $(AGENT_BINARIES)

BINARIES:=$(STANDARD_BINARIES) $(AGENT_BINARIES) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)

# List of binaries that have their size tested
RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES:=pilot-discovery pilot-agent istioctl envoy ztunnel client server

# agent: enables agent-specific files. Usually this is used to trim dependencies where they would be hard to trim through standard refactoring
# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
# not set vtprotobuf: this adds some performance improvement, but at a binary cost increase that is not worth it for the agent
# notrace: helps with https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/56636 and reduces binary size
AGENT_TAGS=agent,disable_pgv,grpcnotrace,retrynotrace
# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
# vtprotobuf: enables optimized protobuf marshalling.
STANDARD_TAGS=vtprotobuf,disable_pgv

.PHONY: build
build: depend ## Builds all go binaries.
	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(AGENT_BINARIES)

# The build-linux target is responsible for building binaries used within containers.
# This target should be expanded upon as we add more Linux architectures: i.e. build-arm64.
# Then a new build target can be created such as build-container-bin that builds these
# various platform images.
.PHONY: build-linux
build-linux: depend
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)

.PHONY: build-cni
build-cni: depend
	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(CNI_BINARIES)

# Create targets for TARGET_OUT_LINUX/binary
# There are two use cases here:
# * Building all docker images (generally in CI). In this case we want to build everything at once, so they share work
# * Building a single docker image (generally during dev). In this case we just want to build the single binary alone
BUILD_ALL ?= true
define build-linux
.PHONY: $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1))
ifeq ($(BUILD_ALL),true)
$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): build-linux
	@:
else
$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(2) $(1)
endif
endef

$(foreach bin,$(STANDARD_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(STANDARD_TAGS))))
$(foreach bin,$(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(AGENT_TAGS))))

# Create helper targets for each binary, like "pilot-discovery"
# As an optimization, these still build everything
$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),$(shell basename $(bin))): build
ifneq ($(TARGET_OUT_LINUX),$(LOCAL_OUT))
# if we are on linux already, then this rule is handled by build-linux above, which handles BUILD_ALL variable
$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),${LOCAL_OUT}/$(shell basename $(bin))): build
endif

MARKDOWN_LINT_ALLOWLIST=localhost:8080,storage.googleapis.com/istio-artifacts/pilot/,http://ratings.default.svc.cluster.local:9080/ratings

lint-helm-global:
	find manifests -name 'Chart.yaml' -print0 | ${XARGS} -L 1 dirname | xargs -r helm lint

lint: lint-python lint-copyright-banner lint-scripts lint-go lint-dockerfiles lint-markdown lint-yaml lint-licenses lint-helm-global ## Runs all linters.
	@bin/check_samples.sh
	@testlinter
	@envvarlinter istioctl pilot security

go-gen:
	@mkdir -p /tmp/bin
	@PATH="${PATH}":/tmp/bin go generate ./...

refresh-goldens:
	REFRESH_GOLDEN=true go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./operator/... \
		./pkg/bootstrap/... \
		./pkg/kube/inject/... \
		./pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/... \
		./pilot/pkg/security/authz/builder/... \
		./pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller/ambient/... \
		./cni/pkg/iptables/... \
		./cni/pkg/plugin/... \
		./istioctl/pkg/workload/... \
		./istioctl/pkg/writer/envoy/configdump/... \
		./istioctl/pkg/writer/ztunnel/configdump/...

update-golden: refresh-goldens

gen-addons:
	manifests/addons/gen.sh

gen: \
	mod-download-go \
	go-gen \
	mirror-licenses \
	format \
	update-crds \
	proto \
	copy-templates \
	gen-addons \
	update-golden ## Update all generated code.

gen-check: gen check-clean-repo

CHARTS = gateway default ztunnel base "gateways/istio-ingress" "gateways/istio-egress" "istio-control/istio-discovery" istio-cni
copy-templates:
	rm manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/*

	# gateway charts
	cp -r manifests/charts/gateways/istio-ingress/templates/* manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates
	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/ingress/egress/g' {} \;
	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/Ingress/Egress/g' {} \;
	if [ -f ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml ]; then \
		sed -i -e 's/"IngressGateways"/"EgressGateways"/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
		sed -i -e 's/istio-ingress/istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
		sed -i -e 's/app: istio-ingress/app: istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
		sed -i -e 's/istio: ingressgateway/istio: egressgateway/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
	fi

	# copy istio-discovery values, but apply some local customizations
	warning=$$(cat manifests/helm-profiles/warning-edit.txt | sed ':a;N;$$!ba;s/\n/\\n/g') ; \
	for chart in $(CHARTS) ; do \
	    rm -rf manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-*.yaml ; \
		for profile in manifests/helm-profiles/*.yaml ; do \
			sed "1s|^|$${warning}\n\n|" $$profile > manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-$$(basename $$profile) ; \
		done; \
		[[ "$$chart" == "ztunnel" ]] || [[ "$$chart" == "gateway" ]] && flatten="true" || flatten="false" ; \
		cat manifests/zzz_profile.yaml | \
		  sed "s/FLATTEN_GLOBALS_REPLACEMENT/$${flatten}/g" \
		  > manifests/charts/$$chart/templates/zzz_profile.yaml ; \
	done

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: go build
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Non-static istioctl targets. These are typically a build artifact.
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64:
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7:
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm GOARM=7 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64:
	GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx:
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64:
	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe:
	GOOS=windows LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl

# generate the istioctl completion files
${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl
	${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion bash > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash

${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl
	${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion zsh > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl

.PHONY: binaries-test
binaries-test:
	go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./tests/binary/... -v --base-dir ${TARGET_OUT} --binaries="$(RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES)"

# istioctl-all makes all of the non-static istioctl executables for each supported OS
.PHONY: istioctl-all
istioctl-all: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64 \
	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx \
	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64 \
	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe

.PHONY: istioctl.completion
istioctl.completion: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl

# istioctl-install builds then installs istioctl into $GOPATH/BIN
# Used for debugging istioctl during dev work
.PHONY: istioctl-install-container
istioctl-install-container: istioctl

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: test
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

.PHONY: test

# This target sets JUNIT_REPORT to the location of the  go-junit-report binary.
# This binary is provided in the build container. If it is not found, the build
# container is not being used, so ask the user to install go-junit-report.
JUNIT_REPORT := $(shell which go-junit-report 2> /dev/null || echo "${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report")

${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report:
	@echo "go-junit-report was not found in the build environment."
	@echo "Please install go-junit-report (ex. go install github.com/jstemmer/go-junit-report@latest)"
	@exit 1

# This is just an alias for racetest now
test: racetest ## Runs all unit tests

# For now, keep a minimal subset. This can be expanded in the future.
BENCH_TARGETS ?= ./pilot/...

PKG ?= ./...
.PHONY: racetest
racetest: $(JUNIT_REPORT)
	go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ${T} -tags=assert -race $(PKG) 2>&1 | tee >($(JUNIT_REPORT) > $(JUNIT_OUT))

.PHONY: benchtest
benchtest: $(JUNIT_REPORT) ## Runs all benchmarks
	prow/benchtest.sh run $(BENCH_TARGETS)
	prow/benchtest.sh compare

report-benchtest:
	prow/benchtest.sh report

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: clean
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: clean

clean: ## Cleans all the intermediate files and folders previously generated.
	rm -rf $(DIRS_TO_CLEAN)

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: docker
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: push

# for now docker is limited to Linux compiles - why ?
include tools/istio-docker.mk

push: docker.push ## Build and push docker images to registry defined by $HUB and $TAG

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: environment and tools
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: show.env show.goenv

show.env: ; $(info $(H) environment variables...)
	$(Q) printenv

show.goenv: ; $(info $(H) go environment...)
	$(Q) $(GO) version
	$(Q) $(GO) env

# show makefile variables. Usage: make show.<variable-name>
show.%: ; $(info $* $(H) $($*))
	$(Q) true

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: custom resource definitions
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

update-crds:
	bin/update_crds.sh

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: artifacts and distribution
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# deb, rpm, etc packages
include tools/packaging/packaging.mk

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: integration tests
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
include tests/integration/tests.mk

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: bookinfo sample
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

export BOOKINFO_VERSION ?= 1.18.0

.PHONY: bookinfo.build bookinfo.push

bookinfo.build:
	@prow/buildx-create
	@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh

bookinfo.push: MULTI_ARCH=true
bookinfo.push:
	@prow/buildx-create
	@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh --push

include common/Makefile.common.mk

    14→
    15→# Our build tools, post jammy, breaks old versions of docker.
    16→# These old versions are surprisingly common, so build in a check here
    17→define warning
    18→Docker version is too old, please upgrade to a newer version.
    19→endef
    20→ifneq ($(findstring google,$(HOSTNAME)),)
    21→warning+=Googlers: go/installdocker\#the-version-of-docker-thats-installed-is-old-eg-1126
    22→endif
    23→# The old docker issue manifests as not being able to run *any* binary. So we can test
    24→# by trying to run a trivial program and ensuring it actually ran. If not, emit our warning.
    25→# Note: we cannot do anything like $(shell docker version) to check, since that would also fail.
    26→CAN_RUN := $(shell echo "can I run echo")
    27→ifeq ($(CAN_RUN),)
    28→$(error $(warning))
    29→endif
    30→
    31→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→# Global Variables
    33→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→ISTIO_GO := $(shell dirname $(realpath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
    35→export ISTIO_GO
    36→SHELL := /bin/bash -o pipefail
    37→
    38→# Version can be defined:
    39→# (1) in a $VERSION shell variable, which takes precedence; or
    40→# (2) in the VERSION file, in which we will append "-dev" to it
    41→ifeq ($(VERSION),)
    42→VERSION_FROM_FILE := $(shell cat VERSION)
    43→ifeq ($(VERSION_FROM_FILE),)
    44→$(error VERSION not detected. Make sure it's stored in the VERSION file or defined in VERSION variable)
    45→endif
    46→VERSION := $(VERSION_FROM_FILE)-dev
    47→endif
    48→
    49→export VERSION
    50→
    51→# Base version of Istio image to use
    52→BASE_VERSION ?= master-2026-02-04T19-02-20
    53→ISTIO_BASE_REGISTRY ?= gcr.io/istio-release
    54→
    55→export GO111MODULE ?= on
    56→export GOPROXY ?= https://proxy.golang.org
    57→export GOSUMDB ?= sum.golang.org
    58→
    59→# If GOPATH is not set by the env, set it to a sane value
    60→GOPATH ?= $(shell cd ${ISTIO_GO}/../../..; pwd)
    61→export GOPATH
    62→
    63→# If GOPATH is made up of several paths, use the first one for our targets in this Makefile
    64→GO_TOP := $(shell echo ${GOPATH} | cut -d ':' -f1)
    65→export GO_TOP
    66→
    67→GO ?= go
    68→
    69→GOARCH_LOCAL := $(TARGET_ARCH)
    70→GOOS_LOCAL := $(TARGET_OS)
    71→
    72→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→# Output control
    74→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→# Invoke make VERBOSE=1 to enable echoing of the command being executed
    76→export VERBOSE ?= 0
    77→# Place the variable Q in front of a command to control echoing of the command being executed.
    78→Q = $(if $(filter 1,$VERBOSE),,@)
    79→# Use the variable H to add a header (equivalent to =>) to informational output
    80→H = $(shell printf "\033[34;1m=>\033[0m")
    81→
    82→ifeq ($(origin DEBUG), undefined)
    83→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
    84→else ifeq ($(DEBUG),0)
    85→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=release
    86→else
    87→  BUILDTYPE_DIR:=debug
    88→  export GCFLAGS:=all=-N -l
    89→  $(info $(H) Build with debugger information)
    90→endif
    91→
    92→# Optional file including user-specific settings (HUB, TAG, etc)
    93→-include .istiorc.mk
    94→
    95→# Environment for tests, the directory containing istio and deps binaries.
    96→# Typically same as GOPATH/bin, so tests work seamlessly with IDEs.
    97→
    98→export ISTIO_BIN=$(GOBIN)
    99→
   100→# If we are running in the Linux build container on non Linux hosts, we add the
   101→# linux binaries to the build dependencies, BUILD_DEPS, which can be added to other targets
   102→# that would need the Linux binaries (ex. tests).
   103→BUILD_DEPS:=
   104→ifeq ($(IN_BUILD_CONTAINER),1)
   105→  ifneq ($(GOOS_LOCAL),"linux")
   106→    BUILD_DEPS += build-linux
   107→  endif
   108→endif
   109→
   110→export ARTIFACTS ?= $(TARGET_OUT)
   111→export JUNIT_OUT ?= $(ARTIFACTS)/junit.xml
   112→export REPO_ROOT := $(shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
   113→
   114→# Make directories needed by the build system
   115→$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX))
   116→$(shell mkdir -p $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/logs)
   117→$(shell mkdir -p $(dir $(JUNIT_OUT)))
   118→
   119→# Need separate target for init:
   120→$(TARGET_OUT):
   121→	@mkdir -p $@
   122→
   123→# If the hub is not explicitly set, use default to istio.
   124→HUB ?=istio
   125→ifeq ($(HUB),)
   126→  $(error "HUB cannot be empty")
   127→endif
   128→
   129→# For dockerx builds, allow HUBS which is a space separated list of hubs. Default to HUB.
   130→HUBS ?= $(HUB)
   131→
   132→# If tag not explicitly set in users' .istiorc.mk or command line, default to the git sha.
   133→TAG ?= $(shell git rev-parse --verify HEAD)
   134→ifeq ($(TAG),)
   135→  $(error "TAG cannot be empty")
   136→endif
   137→
   138→PULL_POLICY ?= IfNotPresent
   139→ifeq ($(TAG),latest)
   140→  PULL_POLICY = Always
   141→endif
   142→ifeq ($(PULL_POLICY),)
   143→  $(error "PULL_POLICY cannot be empty")
   144→endif
   145→
   146→PROW_ARTIFACTS_BASE ?= https://gcsweb.istio.io/gcs/istio-prow
   147→
   148→include tools/proto/proto.mk
   149→
   150→.PHONY: default
   151→default: init build test
   152→
   153→.PHONY: init
   154→# Downloads envoy, based on the SHA defined in the base pilot Dockerfile
   155→init: $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init init-ztunnel-rs
   156→	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/logs
   157→	@mkdir -p ${TARGET_OUT}/release
   158→
   159→# I tried to make this dependent on what I thought was the appropriate
   160→# lock file, but it caused the rule for that file to get run (which
   161→# seems to be about obtaining a new version of the 3rd party libraries).
   162→$(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init: bin/init.sh istio.deps | $(TARGET_OUT)
   163→	@# Add a retry, as occasionally we see transient connection failures to GCS
   164→	@# Like `curl: (56) OpenSSL SSL_read: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL, errno 104`
   165→	TARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) ISTIO_BIN=$(ISTIO_BIN) GOOS_LOCAL=$(GOOS_LOCAL) bin/retry.sh SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL bin/init.sh
   166→	touch $(TARGET_OUT)/istio_is_init
   167→
   168→.PHONY: init-ztunnel-rs
   169→init-ztunnel-rs:
   170→	TARGET_OUT=$(TARGET_OUT) bin/build_ztunnel.sh
   171→
   172→# Pull dependencies such as envoy
   173→depend: init | $(TARGET_OUT)
   174→
   175→DIRS_TO_CLEAN := $(TARGET_OUT)
   176→DIRS_TO_CLEAN += $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)
   177→
   178→$(OUTPUT_DIRS):
   179→	@mkdir -p $@
   180→
   181→.PHONY: ${GEN_CERT}
   182→GEN_CERT := ${ISTIO_BIN}/generate_cert
   183→${GEN_CERT}:
   184→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) && GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) && common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./security/tools/generate_cert
   185→
   186→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   187→# Target: precommit
   188→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   189→.PHONY: precommit format lint
   190→
   191→# Target run by the pre-commit script, to automate formatting and lint
   192→# If pre-commit script is not used, please run this manually.
   193→precommit: format lint
   194→
   195→format: fmt ## Auto formats all code. This should be run before sending a PR.
   196→
   197→fmt: format-go format-python tidy-go
   198→
   199→ifeq ($(DEBUG),1)
   200→# gobuild script uses custom linker flag to set the variables.
   201→RELEASE_LDFLAGS=''
   202→else
   203→RELEASE_LDFLAGS='-extldflags -static -s -w'
   204→endif
   205→
   206→# List of all binaries to build
   207→# We split the binaries into "agent" binaries and standard ones. This corresponds to build "agent".
   208→# This allows conditional compilation to avoid pulling in costly dependencies to the agent, such as XDS and k8s.
   209→AGENT_BINARIES:=./pilot/cmd/pilot-agent
   210→STANDARD_BINARIES:=./istioctl/cmd/istioctl \
   211→  ./pilot/cmd/pilot-discovery \
   212→  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/client \
   213→  ./pkg/test/echo/cmd/server \
   214→  ./samples/extauthz/cmd/extauthz
   215→
   216→CNI_BINARIES:=./cni/cmd/istio-cni \
   217→	./cni/cmd/install-cni
   218→# These are binaries that require Linux to build, and should
   219→# be skipped on other platforms. Notably this includes the current Linux-only Istio CNI plugin
   220→LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES:=$(CNI_BINARIES) \
   221→  $(AGENT_BINARIES)
   222→
   223→BINARIES:=$(STANDARD_BINARIES) $(AGENT_BINARIES) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)
   224→
   225→# List of binaries that have their size tested
   226→RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES:=pilot-discovery pilot-agent istioctl envoy ztunnel client server
   227→
   228→# agent: enables agent-specific files. Usually this is used to trim dependencies where they would be hard to trim through standard refactoring
   229→# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
   230→# not set vtprotobuf: this adds some performance improvement, but at a binary cost increase that is not worth it for the agent
   231→# notrace: helps with https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/56636 and reduces binary size
   232→AGENT_TAGS=agent,disable_pgv,grpcnotrace,retrynotrace
   233→# disable_pgv: disables protoc-gen-validation. This is not used buts adds many MB to Envoy protos
   234→# vtprotobuf: enables optimized protobuf marshalling.
   235→STANDARD_TAGS=vtprotobuf,disable_pgv
   236→
   237→.PHONY: build
   238→build: depend ## Builds all go binaries.
   239→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
   240→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(AGENT_BINARIES)
   241→
   242→# The build-linux target is responsible for building binaries used within containers.
   243→# This target should be expanded upon as we add more Linux architectures: i.e. build-arm64.
   244→# Then a new build target can be created such as build-container-bin that builds these
   245→# various platform images.
   246→.PHONY: build-linux
   247→build-linux: depend
   248→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(STANDARD_TAGS) $(STANDARD_BINARIES)
   249→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES)
   250→
   251→.PHONY: build-cni
   252→build-cni: depend
   253→	GOOS=$(GOOS_LOCAL) GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT)/ -tags=$(AGENT_TAGS) $(CNI_BINARIES)
   254→
   255→# Create targets for TARGET_OUT_LINUX/binary
   256→# There are two use cases here:
   257→# * Building all docker images (generally in CI). In this case we want to build everything at once, so they share work
   258→# * Building a single docker image (generally during dev). In this case we just want to build the single binary alone
   259→BUILD_ALL ?= true
   260→define build-linux
   261→.PHONY: $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1))
   262→ifeq ($(BUILD_ALL),true)
   263→$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): build-linux
   264→	@:
   265→else
   266→$(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/$(shell basename $(1)): $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)
   267→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=$(GOARCH_LOCAL) LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $(TARGET_OUT_LINUX)/ -tags=$(2) $(1)
   268→endif
   269→endef
   270→
   271→$(foreach bin,$(STANDARD_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(STANDARD_TAGS))))
   272→$(foreach bin,$(LINUX_AGENT_BINARIES),$(eval $(call build-linux,$(bin),$(AGENT_TAGS))))
   273→
   274→# Create helper targets for each binary, like "pilot-discovery"
   275→# As an optimization, these still build everything
   276→$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),$(shell basename $(bin))): build
   277→ifneq ($(TARGET_OUT_LINUX),$(LOCAL_OUT))
   278→# if we are on linux already, then this rule is handled by build-linux above, which handles BUILD_ALL variable
   279→$(foreach bin,$(BINARIES),${LOCAL_OUT}/$(shell basename $(bin))): build
   280→endif
   281→
   282→MARKDOWN_LINT_ALLOWLIST=localhost:8080,storage.googleapis.com/istio-artifacts/pilot/,http://ratings.default.svc.cluster.local:9080/ratings
   283→
   284→lint-helm-global:
   285→	find manifests -name 'Chart.yaml' -print0 | ${XARGS} -L 1 dirname | xargs -r helm lint
   286→
   287→lint: lint-python lint-copyright-banner lint-scripts lint-go lint-dockerfiles lint-markdown lint-yaml lint-licenses lint-helm-global ## Runs all linters.
   288→	@bin/check_samples.sh
   289→	@testlinter
   290→	@envvarlinter istioctl pilot security
   291→
   292→go-gen:
   293→	@mkdir -p /tmp/bin
   294→	@PATH="${PATH}":/tmp/bin go generate ./...
   295→
   296→refresh-goldens:
   297→	REFRESH_GOLDEN=true go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./operator/... \
   298→		./pkg/bootstrap/... \
   299→		./pkg/kube/inject/... \
   300→		./pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway/... \
   301→		./pilot/pkg/security/authz/builder/... \
   302→		./pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller/ambient/... \
   303→		./cni/pkg/iptables/... \
   304→		./cni/pkg/plugin/... \
   305→		./istioctl/pkg/workload/... \
   306→		./istioctl/pkg/writer/envoy/configdump/... \
   307→		./istioctl/pkg/writer/ztunnel/configdump/...
   308→
   309→update-golden: refresh-goldens
   310→
   311→gen-addons:
   312→	manifests/addons/gen.sh
   313→
   314→gen: \
   315→	mod-download-go \
   316→	go-gen \
   317→	mirror-licenses \
   318→	format \
   319→	update-crds \
   320→	proto \
   321→	copy-templates \
   322→	gen-addons \
   323→	update-golden ## Update all generated code.
   324→
   325→gen-check: gen check-clean-repo
   326→
   327→CHARTS = gateway default ztunnel base "gateways/istio-ingress" "gateways/istio-egress" "istio-control/istio-discovery" istio-cni
   328→copy-templates:
   329→	rm manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/*
   330→
   331→	# gateway charts
   332→	cp -r manifests/charts/gateways/istio-ingress/templates/* manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates
   333→	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/ingress/egress/g' {} \;
   334→	find ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates -type f -name "*.yaml" ! -name "networkpolicy.yaml" -exec sed -i -e 's/Ingress/Egress/g' {} \;
   335→	if [ -f ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml ]; then \
   336→		sed -i -e 's/"IngressGateways"/"EgressGateways"/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
   337→		sed -i -e 's/istio-ingress/istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
   338→		sed -i -e 's/app: istio-ingress/app: istio-egress/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
   339→		sed -i -e 's/istio: ingressgateway/istio: egressgateway/g' ./manifests/charts/gateways/istio-egress/templates/networkpolicy.yaml; \
   340→	fi
   341→
   342→	# copy istio-discovery values, but apply some local customizations
   343→	warning=$$(cat manifests/helm-profiles/warning-edit.txt | sed ':a;N;$$!ba;s/\n/\\n/g') ; \
   344→	for chart in $(CHARTS) ; do \
   345→	    rm -rf manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-*.yaml ; \
   346→		for profile in manifests/helm-profiles/*.yaml ; do \
   347→			sed "1s|^|$${warning}\n\n|" $$profile > manifests/charts/$$chart/files/profile-$$(basename $$profile) ; \
   348→		done; \
   349→		[[ "$$chart" == "ztunnel" ]] || [[ "$$chart" == "gateway" ]] && flatten="true" || flatten="false" ; \
   350→		cat manifests/zzz_profile.yaml | \
   351→		  sed "s/FLATTEN_GLOBALS_REPLACEMENT/$${flatten}/g" \
   352→		  > manifests/charts/$$chart/templates/zzz_profile.yaml ; \
   353→	done
   354→
   355→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   356→# Target: go build
   357→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   358→
   359→# Non-static istioctl targets. These are typically a build artifact.
   360→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64:
   361→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   362→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7:
   363→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm GOARM=7 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   364→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64:
   365→	GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   366→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx:
   367→	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   368→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64:
   369→	GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   370→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe:
   371→	GOOS=windows LDFLAGS=$(RELEASE_LDFLAGS) common/scripts/gobuild.sh $@ ./istioctl/cmd/istioctl
   372→
   373→# generate the istioctl completion files
   374→${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl
   375→	${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion bash > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash
   376→
   377→${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl: ${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl
   378→	${LOCAL_OUT}/istioctl completion zsh > ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl
   379→
   380→.PHONY: binaries-test
   381→binaries-test:
   382→	go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ./tests/binary/... -v --base-dir ${TARGET_OUT} --binaries="$(RELEASE_SIZE_TEST_BINARIES)"
   383→
   384→# istioctl-all makes all of the non-static istioctl executables for each supported OS
   385→.PHONY: istioctl-all
   386→istioctl-all: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-amd64 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-armv7 ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-linux-arm64 \
   387→	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx \
   388→	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-osx-arm64 \
   389→	${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl-win.exe
   390→
   391→.PHONY: istioctl.completion
   392→istioctl.completion: ${TARGET_OUT}/release/istioctl.bash ${TARGET_OUT}/release/_istioctl
   393→
   394→# istioctl-install builds then installs istioctl into $GOPATH/BIN
   395→# Used for debugging istioctl during dev work
   396→.PHONY: istioctl-install-container
   397→istioctl-install-container: istioctl
   398→
   399→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   400→# Target: test
   401→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   402→
   403→.PHONY: test
   404→
   405→# This target sets JUNIT_REPORT to the location of the  go-junit-report binary.
   406→# This binary is provided in the build container. If it is not found, the build
   407→# container is not being used, so ask the user to install go-junit-report.
   408→JUNIT_REPORT := $(shell which go-junit-report 2> /dev/null || echo "${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report")
   409→
   410→${ISTIO_BIN}/go-junit-report:
   411→	@echo "go-junit-report was not found in the build environment."
   412→	@echo "Please install go-junit-report (ex. go install github.com/jstemmer/go-junit-report@latest)"
   413→	@exit 1
   414→
   415→# This is just an alias for racetest now
   416→test: racetest ## Runs all unit tests
   417→
   418→# For now, keep a minimal subset. This can be expanded in the future.
   419→BENCH_TARGETS ?= ./pilot/...
   420→
   421→PKG ?= ./...
   422→.PHONY: racetest
   423→racetest: $(JUNIT_REPORT)
   424→	go test ${GOBUILDFLAGS} ${T} -tags=assert -race $(PKG) 2>&1 | tee >($(JUNIT_REPORT) > $(JUNIT_OUT))
   425→
   426→.PHONY: benchtest
   427→benchtest: $(JUNIT_REPORT) ## Runs all benchmarks
   428→	prow/benchtest.sh run $(BENCH_TARGETS)
   429→	prow/benchtest.sh compare
   430→
   431→report-benchtest:
   432→	prow/benchtest.sh report
   433→
   434→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   435→# Target: clean
   436→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   437→.PHONY: clean
   438→
   439→clean: ## Cleans all the intermediate files and folders previously generated.
   440→	rm -rf $(DIRS_TO_CLEAN)
   441→
   442→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   443→# Target: docker
   444→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   445→.PHONY: push
   446→
   447→# for now docker is limited to Linux compiles - why ?
   448→include tools/istio-docker.mk
   449→
   450→push: docker.push ## Build and push docker images to registry defined by $HUB and $TAG
   451→
   452→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   453→# Target: environment and tools
   454→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   455→.PHONY: show.env show.goenv
   456→
   457→show.env: ; $(info $(H) environment variables...)
   458→	$(Q) printenv
   459→
   460→show.goenv: ; $(info $(H) go environment...)
   461→	$(Q) $(GO) version
   462→	$(Q) $(GO) env
   463→
   464→# show makefile variables. Usage: make show.<variable-name>
   465→show.%: ; $(info $* $(H) $($*))
   466→	$(Q) true
   467→
   468→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   469→# Target: custom resource definitions
   470→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   471→
   472→update-crds:
   473→	bin/update_crds.sh
   474→
   475→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   476→# Target: artifacts and distribution
   477→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   478→# deb, rpm, etc packages
   479→include tools/packaging/packaging.mk
   480→
   481→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   482→# Target: integration tests
   483→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   484→include tests/integration/tests.mk
   485→
   486→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   487→# Target: bookinfo sample
   488→#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   489→
   490→export BOOKINFO_VERSION ?= 1.18.0
   491→
   492→.PHONY: bookinfo.build bookinfo.push
   493→
   494→bookinfo.build:
   495→	@prow/buildx-create
   496→	@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh
   497→
   498→bookinfo.push: MULTI_ARCH=true
   499→bookinfo.push:
   500→	@prow/buildx-create
   501→	@BOOKINFO_TAG=${BOOKINFO_VERSION} BOOKINFO_HUB=${HUB} samples/bookinfo/src/build-services.sh --push
   502→
   503→include common/Makefile.common.mk
   504→
